title: Not-Your-Average Tomato & Mayo Sarnie
summary: Thick-cut salted tomatoes, drenched in cheesy garlic mayo, sandwiched between slices of hot, toasted white bread. You’ll want another one the second it’s gone.

method.description: Slice the tomatoes into thick 1 cm slices. Place onto a tray and season with salt, set aside (make sure they’re room temp, to get the best flavour.)
Finely grate the cheese and garlic into a bowl. Stir the mayo through and season with plenty of freshly cracked black pepper.

Toast the bread if you fancy. Slather one side of each slice with the cheesy mayo, then layer on the salted tomato slices onto 1 side. Drizzle over a little olive oil, then sandwich together and get stuck in.

tips: Use the ripest, in-season tomatoes you can find – they’re the star.
Feel free to switch up the bread – sourdough or crusty baguette would be great too.
Add a few basil leaves if you want to level it up.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
